items.answer: Here it means a short, intentional comedic audio cue—cartoon impacts, silly whooshes, meme-style stings, or exaggerated foley—authored from text instead of microphones. The goal is a single polished cue you can drop straight into an edit, game build, or social cut without rebuilding sessions from scratch. items.question: How should I write prompts for stronger comedic cues?
items.answer: Lead with the joke mechanic (slip, bonk, rimshot), then add setting (tight room vs. stadium) and performance words (wobbly, dry, brassy). Mention duration cues like "quick sting" or "two-second tail." Specific prompts outperform vague ones—compare "rubber stretch snap" with "goofy tension release" and refine from listener feedback. Students searching funny sound effects free packs for reels should still follow attribution rules when required; brands evaluating funny sound effects free tiers should map seats to editors.
